Terms & Conditions
1. DEFINITION & ESSENCE 1. BANSAL POLYPLAST Pvt. Ltd. herein referred to as the Buyer/ Service Recipient and KDI Power Pvt. Ltd.
referred to as the
Seller/ Service Provider and together as Parties.
2. Supply of Goods and Services shall in all circumstances be strictly in accordance with Buyer's/ Service
Recipient's specifications, Terms and Conditions.
3. All Purchase Orders shall be strictly subject to Buyer's / Service Recipient's standard terms and conditions for
purchase of goods/ services as mentioned below, unless specifically mentioned in the Purchase Order/ Work order

3. PAYMENT TERMS 20% Payment of the basic value in advance,
60% Payment along with GST before dispatch of material
20% Payment within 3 Months from the date of delivery at site.
The quoted prices shall remain firm and fixed for the entire duration of the supply and shall not be subjected to
escalation of any kind, unless specified in the purchase Order/contract.
The price payable for the Goods shall be that stated in the Order and, unless otherwise stated, shall be inclusive of
all charges including, but not limited to packaging material, packing, shipping, loading, carriage, insurance and
delivery of the Goods to the delivery address and any duties, imposts, levies or taxes if any.
Buyer's payment process will be by E -payment and RTGS/NEFT charges, if any, will be borne by the Seller.

4. TAXES & DUTIES 1. GST extra as applicable.
2. Seller shall ensure that the HSN code pertaining to the supplied items shall be mentioned in the invoices, failing
which the invoice shall be returned to the vendor and will be processed only upon receipt of the corrected invoice,
incorporating the HSN codes.
3. Seller should mention theirs and Buyer's GSTIN number in all invoices and corresponding documents.
4. The Seller shall further ensure that original invoices should be provided at the time of delivering the materials.

6. DELIVERY SCHEDULE 15 days from date of Issuance of PO/Acceptance of prepayment.
Timely delivery is essential for this Order.
All materials must be delivered in one lot unless specified and mutually agreed between both Buyer and Seller. In
the event of delayed delivery, uninformed staggered delivery or any other issues, Buyer reserves the right to cancel
the Order or impose penalty on Seller at any time.
The Seller shall ensure that each delivery is accompanied by a delivery note which is prominently displayed and
which shows, inter alia, the Order number, date of Order, number of packages and contents and, in the case of
part delivery, the outstanding balance remaining to be delivered.
The Goods shall be delivered as per the incoterms specified in the Order and at the time and in the manner
specified in the Order.
The Buyer may specify in writing to the Seller at any time before delivery, another time place or manner for
delivery, in which case that other time, place or manner applies in place of those stated in the Order.
Where access to premises is necessary in connection with delivery, the Seller and its Sub-Contractors shall at all
times comply with the requirements of the Buyer's authorized representative.
The Buyer will not be required to pay for any Goods which are delivered to the Buyer in excess of the quantities
Ordered by the Buyer. Any such excess will be and will remain at the Seller's risk and will be returnable at the
Seller's expense.
If the goods are dispatched through a mode of transport and/or carrier not approved by Buyer, any incidence of
demurrage and wharfage shall be debited to the Seller's A/c.

16. INDEMNITY To the fullest extent permitted by law, the Supplier agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend the Buyer and
its affiliated companies, their customers, directors, officers, employees and agents, from and against any loss,
liability, cost, expenses, suits actions claims and all other obligations and proceedings whatsoever, including
without limitation, all judgements rendered against and all fines and penalties imposed upon the Buyer, and any
reasonable attorney's fees and any other costs of litigation (hereinafter collectively collectively referred to as
"Liabilities") arising out of death, personal injury and/or damage to property caused by the Supplier, its
employees, agents, subcontractors, or in any way attributable to the Goods.
